Apply to a new repository

Add these LabVIEW CI capabilities to another repository. Pick what you want — get an install command and a ready-to-commit bundle.

← Back to dashboard

1. Your repository

Where to install. Required for the one-click install; the command-line option can also infer it from your git remote.

2. Capabilities

Recommended ones are pre-selected. Dependencies are added automatically.

3. Install

Let the configurator open a pull request that adds these files to your repository — nothing to download, unzip, or commit by hand. The first time, you paste a one-time fine-grained token; the panel walks you through creating it with the permissions pre-filled.

One-time setup — a token to open the install pull request

  1. Create a fine-grained token ↗ — the required permissions are pre-filled.
  2. Repository access → Only select repositories → add your-org/your-repo.
  3. Confirm Permissions: Contents, Pull requests, and Workflows each set to Read and write (Workflows is what lets the install add files under .github/workflows/).
  4. Generate, then paste it below.

Stored only in this browser (localStorage); sent only to api.github.com — never to the page or to CI. Revoke it any time once the pull request is open.

  1. Review and merge pull request #….
  2. Set Workflow permissions to Read and write (Settings › Actions › General).
  3. After the first run publishes the gh-pages branch, enable GitHub Pages from it (Settings › Pages).
Other ways to install (command line or download)

Run this from the root of your repository. It only writes files into your working tree — review with git diff before committing.

macOS / Linux / Git Bash
Windows PowerShell

          

          

            
            
            
          

        

      

    


    

      
4. Finish setup

      
    
        
  1. Merge the install pull request (or, if you used the manual option, commit the added .github/ files).
    2. 
        
  2. Enable GitHub Pages from the gh-pages branch (Settings › Pages).
    3. 
        
  3. Set Workflow permissions to Read and write (Settings › Actions › General).
    4. 
        
  4. Optionally pin settings as Actions variables (shown after you choose options).
    5. 
        
  5. Open a pull request that changes a VI — the pipeline runs and reports back.
    6. 
      

      
None of these choices are permanent — change the LabVIEW version, platforms, activities, and container dependencies any time from ⚙ Configure Workers on the dashboard (it opens a pull request with your changes). To pull later tooling improvements, re-run the installer with --update.
